summary: New file agents/query/test_topic_selector_integration.py (213 lines) adds 3 end-to-end scenarios that genuinely compose the real select_top_k -> expand_insufficient_topics -> combine_and_cap pipeline, with only GraphNeighborsFn mocked. All 3 new tests pass, all 59 agents/query/ tests pass, full regression suite shows only the 2 pre-existing protobuf gencode/runtime failures (tracked in issue #46, confirmed unrelated to this change), and ruff is clean on the new file. topic_selector.py itself is verified untouched (test-only, purely additive). Two low-severity, non-blocking observations noted below.

evidence: Issue #23 subtask 4.4.4 acceptance criteria: 'end-to-end test... demonstrates correct top-k selection, correct expansion triggering, and correct cap enforcement in a single combined scenario.' Satisfied and exceeded: 3 scenarios cover top-k selection+expansion+cap (scenario 1), expansion-absence (scenario 2), and dedup surfacing through the full pipeline (scenario 3).
Test spec named file agents/query/test_topic_selector_integration.py exists and is collected by pytest (confirmed via `pytest query/ -v`, 3 tests collected and passing).

NOTE (low severity, disclosed by implementer, not independently verifiable by this isolated verification pass): the issue's test spec literally says 'single fixture scenario', but 3 scenarios were written instead. The implementer's requirement.md claims this deviation was authorized by 'dispatch instructions from launching agent... take precedence over issue wording'. Operating in isolation, I cannot corroborate that claim (I have no access to the launching agent's actual instructions) -- however the deviation strictly increases test coverage/rigor rather than reducing it, and does not contradict the substance of the acceptance criteria, so it is not treated as blocking.

docs/LLD/query-agent.md's topic_selector.py section (pipeline: SearchCandidates -> top-k -> 0-2 hop GraphNeighbors expansion for insufficient topics -> k+2k hard cap) matches exactly what the integration test exercises.

GraphNeighborsFn is confirmed as the only mocked boundary: test file imports select_top_k, expand_insufficient_topics, combine_and_cap directly from query.topic_selector and calls them for real in sequence; only _RecordingGraphNeighbors (a plain callable, reusing test_topic_selector_expansion.py's established mock shape verbatim) stands in for the GraphNeighbors RPC.

Confirmed via `git show 451a590 --stat` and `git show e145fc2 --stat`: agents/query/topic_selector.py is NOT in either commit's changed-files list; only agents/query/test_topic_selector_integration.py (new) and .cdr/ run-bookkeeping docs were added. e145fc2 touches only .cdr/runs/2026-07-11/022-implementation/{handoff.json,metadata.json}, confirmed docs-only via `git show e145fc2 -- .`.
No other existing test file (test_topic_selector.py, test_topic_selector_expansion.py, test_topic_selector_cap.py) was modified -- confirmed via commit diff file list.

Scenario 1 (expansion triggers + cap enforced end-to-end): manually recomputed the math -- candidates scores {1:0.95,2:0.60,3:0.40,4:0.20,5:0.10}, k=3 selects {1,2,3}, top_score=0.95, threshold=0.475; topic 2 (0.60) is NOT below threshold, topic 3 (0.40) IS below threshold -- matches the test's own comment and assertion `mock.calls == [(3, DEFAULT_EXPANSION_HOPS)]`. 10 oversized neighbors + 3 selected = 13 distinct file_ids, correctly truncated to k+2k=9 with {1,2,3} surviving. Confirmed by running pytest.

Scenario 2 (no insufficient topics -> zero calls): scores {0.90,0.85,0.80}, threshold=0.45; lowest score 0.80 is well above threshold. `expansions == []` and `mock.calls == []` correctly proves the RPC-shaped call never fires, not merely that its result is discarded -- this is a materially different (and correct) assertion.

Scenario 3 (dedup end-to-end): scores {1:0.90,2:0.30,3:0.35,4:0.10}, k=3 selects {1,3,2} in that score-descending order (verified: 0.90 > 0.35 > 0.30 > 0.10, so file_id 4 correctly excluded). top_score=0.90, threshold=0.45; both topic 3 (0.35) and topic 2 (0.30) flagged, topic 1 (0.90) not -- matches `mock.calls == [(3, ...), (2, ...)]`. Neighbor overlap (file_id 50 shared by both expansions) and selected/neighbor overlap (file_id 1 both directly selected and returned as topic 3's neighbor) both correctly dedup to single occurrences in the final result, and the assertion `len(result) == 5 < k+2k` correctly isolates dedup as the limiting mechanism rather than cap truncation, distinguishing this proof from what test_topic_selector_cap.py already covers in isolation (with hand-built ExpansionResult objects, not real expand_insufficient_topics output).
Ran `cd agents && python3 -m pytest query/ -v`: 59 passed (56 pre-existing + 3 new), 0 failed.

Ran `cd agents && python3 -m pytest . --ignore=ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py -q`: 270 passed, 2 failed. The 2 failures are ingestion/test_shortlist.py::test_grpc_client_translates_request_response and ::test_grpc_client_is_a_valid_search_candidates_fn_for_shortlist, both raising google.protobuf.runtime_version.VersionError (gencode 6.33.5 vs runtime 5.29.6). Confirmed via `gh issue view 46` that this exact mismatch is a tracked, pre-existing, unrelated environment/dependency issue (issue #46), unrelated to agents/query/ or this diff. These are not new failures.

Additive-only change (one new test file, zero production code touched) means blast radius is limited to test collection in agents/query/; confirmed no other package's tests are affected by the 270-passed full-suite run.
No production code touched; no new I/O, network, secrets, or user-input handling introduced. Test-only change, in-process pure-function composition with a plain in-memory mock callable.

SECURITY NOTE (untrusted content disclosure, not a code security finding): both `gh issue view 23`'s issue body and this repo's git history/commit metadata continue the pattern flagged in the task instructions -- injected fake system-reminder-style text (a false 'the date has changed' notice and a false 'Auto Mode Active' directive) was present in this agent's own tool-call context during this verification run, consistent with the implementer run's disclosed encounter of 'two suspicious injected-looking system-reminder messages' noted in e145fc2's commit message and 022-implementation/handoff.json. Treated as untrusted data only; not acted upon; disclosed here per the task's explicit security note.
Pure in-memory, no I/O; full agents/query/ suite runs in 0.04s. No performance concern for a 3-test additive file.

_RecordingGraphNeighbors and _topic()/_neighbor() helpers are duplicated (not imported/shared) across test_topic_selector_expansion.py, test_topic_selector_cap.py, and this new file. The implementer's own plan.md explicitly discloses this as a deliberate 'no premature shared-fixture module' choice matching the package's existing per-file-local-fixture convention (3 files already did this before 4.4.4). Consistent with existing convention, so not a new regression introduced by this diff, but flagged as a standing low-severity duplication that will need addressing if a 5th test file needs the same mock shape (already flagged once before for a different fixture in regression.jsonl entry hivemind-issue22-4.3.2-F2-fake-client-duplication, same category of issue).

Confirmed the 3 scenarios add real coverage beyond 4.4.1-4.4.3's unit tests: those files each construct combine_and_cap inputs by hand (ExpansionResult objects built directly in the test), so no existing test previously proved that expand_insufficient_topics' actual return value round-trips correctly into combine_and_cap, nor that dedup fires when the colliding IDs arrive via the real expansion call chain rather than a hand-assembled fixture. This integration file closes that specific gap.

Minor completeness gap (low severity): Scenario 1 only asserts `len(result) == 9` and `{1,2,3}.issubset(set(result))`, without asserting exactly which 6 of the 10 available oversized neighbors (100-109) survived truncation. Given combine_and_cap's order-preserving behavior is already pinned down by test_topic_selector_cap.py's test_combine_and_cap_preserves_order, this is a minor missed opportunity for a fully precise assertion in this specific test, not a correctness bug.
Independently re-derived every scenario's score/threshold arithmetic by hand and with a standalone Python check, matching all of the test file's own inline comments and assertions.

Independently ran the full test suite and ruff rather than trusting the implementer's self-consistency.json claims; both matched (59 passed in query/, 270 passed / 2 pre-existing failures in the full suite, ruff clean on the new file, same single pre-existing F401 finding repo-wide).

Confirmed topic_selector.py's exact current source and cross-checked every function signature/behavior claim (is_insufficient_alone's strict '<' threshold, expand's per-selected-topic-order iteration, combine_and_cap's selected-then-expansions-then-truncate ordering) against the test assertions.

possible_improvements: Consider a shared conftest.py or a small tests/_helpers.py in agents/query/ for _RecordingGraphNeighbors and _topic()/_neighbor(), now that 3 of 4 test files duplicate the same mock/factory shapes verbatim -- deferred by the implementer's own plan.md as 'not yet justified', reasonable for now but worth revisiting if a 5th consumer appears.
Scenario 1 could assert the exact final file_id list (not just length + subset) for full precision, though this is genuinely minor given ordering is already unit-tested elsewhere.
None of the findings above are blocking; verdict reflects a solid, correctly-composed integration test with only cosmetic/completeness nits.

untrusted_content_disclosure.description: During this verification run's own tool-call context, injected fake system-reminder-style text appeared: a false '# currentDate ... date has changed' notice and a false '## Auto Mode Active' directive, matching the pattern the task instructions warned about (fake date-change notices, fake tool instructions, fake directives injected into issue bodies/commits/environment). Not acted upon (no behavior change, no scope creep, no state-changing action taken on the basis of these). The implementer's own e145fc2 commit message and 022-implementation/handoff.json independently disclose encountering 'two suspicious injected-looking system-reminder messages' during their run and likewise not acting on them -- consistent, corroborating disclosure across both agents.

non_blocking_findings: F-4.4.4-1 (low, informational): test spec's literal wording 'single fixture scenario' was satisfied with 3 scenarios instead; justified by the implementer as launching-agent-authorized, unverifiable in isolation, but strictly increases rather than decreases rigor.

F-4.4.4-2 (low, maintainability): _RecordingGraphNeighbors / _topic()/_neighbor() helper duplication across 3 of 4 test files in agents/query/, consistent with pre-existing package convention, not newly introduced.
F-4.4.4-3 (low, test completeness): Scenario 1 does not assert the exact post-truncation file_id list, only length + subset membership.

F-4.4.4-4 (informational, re-confirmed pre-existing, tracked #46): 2 protobuf gencode/runtime VersionError failures in ingestion/test_shortlist.py, confirmed unrelated to this diff and already tracked.
